_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural controls required to enforce data access, privacy, and residency requirements by design.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Knowledge SP-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-010 (RC, cosine: 0.506), AI-T-020 (AI, cosine: 0.492).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.588). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, requirements]. NICE-only terms [information, priority] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, architectural, classification, controls, data, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.912, R=0.866). P exceeds R by 0.046 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of priority information requirements" and "Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural co" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.511 < STS 0.588 (gap -0.077). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [information, priority] and [access, architectural, classification] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.3%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, requirements]. NICE-only: [information, priority]. WIDAI-only: [access, architectural, classification, controls, data, design].
